contract.name: v2/disaster/agency/loans.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster and emergency loan spending by toptier agency and, when loan award types are provided, exposes subtier children with the same metrics.

filter.description: Defines the disaster scope and optional refinements for loan-only aggregations.

def_codes.description: Non-empty array of Disaster Emergency Fund (DEFC) codes to include.
def_codes.constraints: Values must be chosen from the allowed DEFC list returned by the API; invalid entries yield HTTP 400 with the accepted codes. Empty arrays return HTTP 422.

award_type_codes.description: Optional list of loan award type codes.
award_type_codes.constraints: Only '07' and/or '08' are accepted; any other value returns HTTP 400. When present, responses may include subtier agency children and an 'id' sort warning in messages.

query.description: Optional keyword filter applied to agency descriptions when used with loan award types.
query.constraints: Must be a non-empty string. Using query without loan award_type_codes causes an HTML 500 response; empty strings return HTTP 422.

pagination.description: Optional pagination and sorting controls; defaults are page 1, limit 10, sort 'id', order 'desc'.

limit.constraints: Accepted range is 1-100 inclusive. Values below 1 or above 100 return HTTP 422.

page.constraints: Must be >= 1. Zero returns HTTP 422. Pages beyond the available results return HTTP 200 with an empty results array and hasPrevious=true.

order.constraints: Allowed values: 'asc', 'desc'. Any other value returns HTTP 400 with a detail message.

sort.constraints: Allowed values: id, code, description, award_count, obligation, outlay, face_value_of_loan. Requests using 'id' are accepted but the API sorts by description and emits a warning message. Invalid values return HTTP 400 listing the accepted options.

spending_type.description: Optional metric selector such as 'total', 'obligation', or 'face_value_of_loan'.
spending_type.constraints: The parameter is accepted but had no observable effect on totals or ordering in any probe.

totals.description: Aggregate metrics across all matched agencies.

award_count.description: Total number of loan awards; observed as integer values (may be 0).

obligation.description: Sum of loan obligations across the filtered agencies.

outlay.description: Sum of loan outlays across the filtered agencies.

face_value_of_loan.description: Total face value of the filtered loan awards.

results.description: List of agency aggregates. Each entry includes numeric id, agency code string, description, award_count, obligation, outlay, face_value_of_loan, and a children array for subtier agencies (usually empty unless loan award types are supplied).

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata mirroring the request and result set size (page, total agency count, limit, optional next/previous numbers, hasNext, hasPrevious flags).

messages.description: Optional warning strings. Observed when sort='id' is requested, indicating the fallback to description ordering.

messages: Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field.

quirks: Requests that include sort:'id' return results ordered by description and add an explanatory string to the messages array.
Keyword searches must include loan award_type_codes; omitting them triggers a 500 HTML error.
The spending_type parameter is currently inert despite being accepted.
page_metadata.total reports the number of matched agencies, not the number of pages.
Responses set Set-Cookie headers even though the API is unauthenticated.
risks: Using query without loan award_type_codes returns an HTML 500 error, which will break JSON parsers unless handled explicitly.
Sorting by 'id' silently falls back to description ordering; clients expecting numeric id ordering may misinterpret result sequences.
The service occasionally resets the connection (including the initial IPv6 attempt); callers should implement retries and prefer IPv4 where possible.
Responses include Set-Cookie headers that integrators should ignore to avoid unintended sticky sessions.
gaps: The effect of spending_type values remains unknown; no probe observed a change in the aggregation.

probes.notes: Minimal request with required def_codes returned aggregated totals for a single agency.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"totals":{"obligation":0.0,...},"results":[],"messages":["Notice! API Request to sort on 'id' field isn't fully implemented..."]}

probes.notes: Keyword searches work when paired with loan award types and emit the sort warning.
Using query without loan award_type_codes results in an HTML 500 error page.
Invalid loan award type codes are rejected with HTTP 400 and the accepted values.
pagination.limit enforces a minimum value of 1.
Invalid DEFC codes return HTTP 400 with the allowed value list.

mismatches: Docs describe Result.id as a string, but live responses use numeric ids.
Docs treat query as a generic optional string, yet the API returns a 500 HTML error unless loan award_type_codes are supplied.
Docs advertise sort 'id' as implemented, but the API falls back to description ordering and emits a warning.
gaps: The effect of spending_type values remains unknown; no probe observed a change in the aggregation.
risks: Using query without loan award_type_codes returns an HTML 500 error, which will break JSON parsers unless handled explicitly.
Sorting by 'id' silently falls back to description ordering; clients expecting numeric id ordering may misinterpret result sequences.
The service occasionally resets the connection (including the initial IPv6 attempt); callers should implement retries and prefer IPv4 where possible.
Responses include Set-Cookie headers that integrators should ignore to avoid unintended sticky sessions.
